stimulus: Lateral antibiotic-resistance gene transfer occurs when a bacterium of one strain acquires genetic material from a bacterium of another strain through nonreproductive means. The genetic material can then be passed along “vertically” in the second strain—that is, through ordinary cell division. Microbiologist Lakshmi Chandran and her team have hypothesized infection by bacteriophages as a mechanism of resistance-gene transfer between bacterial strains found in soil: while reproducing in a host cell, a phage could pick up a resistance gene from one strain, then move on to a cell of a different strain and deposit the gene into it in turn.
stem: Which choice best describes the function of the underlined portion in the text as a whole?

answerOptions.content: It describes a means by which lateral antibiotic-resistance gene transfer might occur in soil bacteria.
It explains why bacteriophages are less susceptible to gene transfer than their hosts are.
It clarifies why some genes are more likely to be transferred laterally than others are.
It contrasts how lateral gene transfer occurs in soil bacteria with how it occurs in marine bacteria.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The text defines lateral antibiotic-resistance gene transfer and proposes infection by bacteriophages as a mechanism between soil bacteria. The underlined portion describes how that mechanism could work.

Choice B is incorrect because the underlined portion describes phages as the agent that does the transferring, not as more or less susceptible than hosts. Choice C is incorrect because the text never discusses which genes are more likely to be transferred. Choice D is incorrect because the text never discusses lateral gene transfer in marine bacteria.
